properties.remark: CHP REPORTED FLOODING ON THE ROADWAY BETWEEN SR190 AND SR136 ON THE WAY TO DEATH VALLEY. THE ROAD WAS CLOSED BUT THEY ARE ON THE WAY TO REOPEN IT AND WARN DRIVERS TO BE CAUTIOUS.

*** 1 FATAL *** FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED IN AND AROUND YUCCA VALLEY. MULTIPLE STREETS WERE FLOODED BY WATER, MUD, AND DEBRIS. FOUR HOMES WERE FLOODED. SEVERAL SWIFT WATER RESCUES WERE PERFORMED. ONE PERSON DIED WHEN RAPIDLY RISING WATER OVERTOOK A VEHICLE.
A VEHICLE WAS STUCK IN MUD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SUNBURST STREET AND HILLTOP DRIVE. AN HOUR LATER, THE INTERSECTION OF SUNBURST STREET AND OLEANDER DRIVE WAS COMPLETELY FLOODED AND CLOSED.

SEVERAL REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING IN THE MORONGO BASIN. HIGHWAY 62 WAS FLOODED IN MULTIPLE AREAS FROM MORONGO VALLEY TO YUCCA VALLEY. BELLA VISTA RD IN MORONGO VALLEY WAS FLOODED WITH WATER ALSO ENTERING A HOME. FLOODING CONTINUED AT LEAST THROUGH 830 AND PROBABLY LONGER.

TRAINED SPOTTERS REPORTED STRONG WINDS IN TOPOCK, AZ. POWER OUTAGE REPORTED IN TOPOCK AND UTILITY COMPANY IS RESPONDING TO LINES DOWN. ALSO RECEIVED A VIDEO VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF STRONG WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN WITH ISOLATED FLOODING IN TOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

*** 1 INJ *** CHP REPORTS A SEMI TRUCK ROLL OVER SOUTH OF FIVE MILE ROAD ON HIGHWAY 95 NEAR NEEDLES, AROUND MILE MARKER 51. LAW ENFORCEMENT TWITTER CONFIRMS THAT TRUCK ROLLED DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. REPORTS OF GUSTS AROUND 40 MPH WITH THE STORM AT THE TIME. A MINOR INJURY WAS REPORTED.

THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 50 TO 60 MPH CAUSED SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE TO BOTH THE THOMPSON BAY AND SOUTH BASIN AREAS. SIXTEEN PEOPLE HAD TO BE RESCUED FROM 3 DIFFERENT BOATS DUE TO THE STRONG WINDS AND WAVES NEAR THE MOUTH OF THE RIVER IN THE NORTH AREA OF LAKE HAVASU. THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO BOTH BOATS AND DOCKS AT THE NAUTICAL BEACHFRONT RESORT. SEVERAL BOATS TIED TO THE DOCKS WERE SUNK WHILE SEVERAL OTHER RECEIVED SEVERE DAMAGE. IN ADDITION, TWO 70 FOOT HOUSE BOATS SUFFERED EXTREME DAMAGE. THE COURTESY DOCKS AT THE RESORT ALSO RECEIVED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. EARLY INDICATIONS SHOW THAT THERE IS SEVERAL HUNDREDS OF THOUSANDS OF DOLLARS IN DAMAGE.
A NEW STORAGE FACILITY BEING BUILT COLLAPSED DUE TO THE STRONG WINDS. THIS BUILDING WAS A 2 STORY METAL FRAME AT THE TIME. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.
SPOTTER REPORTS A DUST STORM IN LAKE HAVASU CITY ALONG WILLOW AVENUE NEAR AZ 95 WITH VISIBILITY TO NEAR ZERO.

LAKE MEAD PARK RANGERS REPORTED THAT STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS OVERNIGHT CAUSED A FLOATING BATHROOM TO BREAK LOOSE FROM ITS ANCHOR AND WAS PUSHED INTO A DOCK CAUSING MINOR DAMAGE.
